devanagari: आदित्यव्यूहविवरणम् । अथेममर्थं पृच्छामो भवन्तं बहुवित्तमम् । समस्ततन्त्रराद्धान्ते भवान् भागवततत्त्ववित् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: ādityavyūhavivaraṇamathemamarthaṃ pṛcchāmo bhavantaṃ bahuvittamamsamastatantrarāddhānte bhavān bhāgavatatattvavit
translation:
Śaunaka said:
O (great) devotee of the Lord! You are the most prominent among the versatiles (knowers of different Śāstras). You know the heart—the secret conclusions of the scriptures of different works on Tantra. (Our forebear Mārkaṇḍeya was expert in the Tāntric way of worshipping the Lord. We want to know the tāntric way he adopted for visualization of the Lord. Hence, we request your worship to discourse upon the following subject:

devanagari: तान्त्रिकाः परिचर्यायां केवलस्य श्रियः पतेः । अङ्गोपाङ्गायुधाकल्पं कल्पयन्ति यथा च यैः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tāntrikāḥ paricaryāyāṃ kevalasya śriyaḥ pateḥaṅgopāṅgāyudhākalpaṃ kalpayanti yathā ca yaiḥ
translation: The Lord of goddess Śrī is essentially pure consciousness (and pure sattva). But when experts in (and authors of) Tantras deal with meditation and process of worshipping the Lord, they conceive him as possessed of limbs of the body (e.g. hands and feet) attended upon (by Garuḍa), armed with weapons (like the discus Sudarśana), adorned with precious stones and ornaments (like the Kaustubha gem).

devanagari: तन्नो वर्णय भद्रं ते क्रियायोगं बुभुत्सताम् । येन क्रियानैपुणेन मर्त्यो यायादमर्त्यताम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: tanno varṇaya bhadraṃ te kriyāyogaṃ bubhutsatāmyena kriyānaipuṇena martyo yāyādamartyatām
translation: O Sūta I We are anxious to know the Tāntric course of active worship by becoming thoroughly proficient in which a mortal may attain immortality (Mokṣa as the sage Mārkaṇḍeya did). May God bless you. But be pleased to elucidate of what principles are they (limbs, attendants etc.) presumed to be constituted (and the origin of this conception).

devanagari: सूत उवाच । नमस्कृत्य गुरून्वक्ष्ये विभूतीर्वैष्णवीरपि । याः प्रोक्ता वेदतन्त्राभ्यामाचार्यैः पद्मजादिभिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācanamaskṛtya gurūnvakṣye vibhūtīrvaiṣṇavīrapiyāḥ proktā vedatantrābhyāmācāryaiḥ padmajādibhiḥ
translation:
Sūta said:
Having first bowed to my preceptors, I shall describe to you the various glorious manifestations of Lord Viṣṇu (such as the cosmic form—virāṭ) even though it is difficult to comprehend them—Forms which are (to some extent) described in details by (authoritative Tantras) teachers like the lotus- born god Brahmā and others (like Śaṅkara, Nārada) in the light of the Vedas and texts (saṃhitās), Tantras.

devanagari: मायाद्यैर्नवभिस्तत्त्वैः स विकारमयो विराट् । निर्मितो दृश्यते यत्र सचित्के भुवनत्रयम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: māyādyairnavabhistattvaiḥ sa vikāramayo virāṭnirmito dṛśyate yatra sacitke bhuvanatrayam
translation: That celebrated cosmic form—Virāṭ—consisting of the sixteen modifications (viz. mind, five cognitive senses, five conative organs and five gross elements—mahābhūtas) is evolved out of nine principles such as Māyā (or Prakṛti) and others eight, viz. mahat (the principle of cosmic intelligence), sūtra (the active aspect of mahat), ahaṅkāra (the cosmic ego) and five tan-mātrās (subtle elements). When the spirit or the intelligent Principle presides over it to activate it the three worlds make their appearance.

devanagari: एतद्वै पौरुषं रूपं भूः पादौ द्यौः शिरो नभः । नाभिः सूर्योऽक्षिणी नासे वायुः कर्णौ दिशः प्रभोः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: etadvai pauruṣaṃ rūpaṃ bhūḥ pādau dyauḥ śiro nabhaḥnābhiḥ sūryo'kṣiṇī nāse vāyuḥ karṇau diśaḥ prabhoḥ
translation: This verily constitutes the Puruṣa or Virāṭ (Cosmic) form. Of that Lord, the earth is regarded as his feet, the heavens, the head; the mid-air region, the navel; the sun, the eyes; vāyu (wind or air) his nostrils; cardinal points, as his ears.

devanagari: प्रजापतिः प्रजननमपानो मृत्युरीशितुः । तद्बाहवो लोकपाला मनश्चन्द्रो भ्रुवौ यमः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpatiḥ prajananamapāno mṛtyurīśituḥtadbāhavo lokapālā manaścandro bhruvau yamaḥ
translation: His organ of creation, Prajāpati (the presiding deity of creation); the anus of the Lord is the (god of) death; the guardians of the spheres, his arms; the Moon (its presiding deity) is the mind; Yama, the eye-brows.

devanagari: लज्जोत्तरोऽधरो लोभो दन्ता ज्योत्स्ना स्मयो भ्रमः । रोमाणि भूरुहा भूम्नो मेघाः पुरुषमूर्धजाः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: lajjottaro'dharo lobho dantā jyotsnā smayo bhramaḥromāṇi bhūruhā bhūmno meghāḥ puruṣamūrdhajāḥ
translation: Modesty or Shyness, his upper lip; greed, his lower lip; the moonlight represents the (rightness of his) teeth; illusion is his smile; trees (i.e. the plant world), the hair on his body, and the clouds form the locks on the head of that Supreme Person.

devanagari: यावानयं वै पुरुषो यावत्या संस्थया मितः । तावानसावपि महापुरुषो लोकसंस्थया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvānayaṃ vai puruṣo yāvatyā saṃsthayā mitaḥtāvānasāvapi mahāpuruṣo lokasaṃsthayā
translation: According as the body of an individual human being is measured with reference to the dimensions and dispositions of the parts of his body, the dispositions and proportion of the personality of the Supreme Person are to be regarded similarly with reference to the spheres in the universe. (Thus a man is seven spans in height, the seven worlds such as bhūr, bhuvaḥ, etc. are to be presumed such ‘spans’ in the case of the cosmic person.)

devanagari: कौस्तुभव्यपदेशेन स्वात्मज्योतिर्बिभर्त्यजः । तत्प्रभा व्यापिनी साक्षात्श्रीवत्समुरसा विभुः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: kaustubhavyapadeśena svātmajyotirbibhartyajaḥtatprabhā vyāpinī sākṣātśrīvatsamurasā vibhuḥ
translation: The eternal (lit. unborn) Lord wears the pure consciousness of the Jīva unḍer the name Kaustubha and the splendour radiated from it is the well-known streak of golden hair—Śrī-vatsa on his (right part of the) bosom.

devanagari: स्वमायां वनमालाख्यां नानागुणमयीं दधत् । वासश्छन्दोमयं पीतं ब्रह्मसूत्रं त्रिवृत्स्वरम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: svamāyāṃ vanamālākhyāṃ nānāguṇamayīṃ dadhatvāsaśchandomayaṃ pītaṃ brahmasūtraṃ trivṛtsvaram
translation: He wears the Māyā constituted of many guṇas (Sattva, rajas, etc.) as the Vana-mālā (garland of five kinds of special flowers). His yellow silken garment (round his loins) is constituted of the Vedas; his sacred thread is the sacred syllable OM consisting of three syllables A+U+M.

devanagari: बिभर्ति साङ्ख्यं योगं च देवो मकरकुण्डले । मौलिं पदं पारमेष्ठ्यं सर्वलोकाभयङ्करम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bibharti sāṅkhyaṃ yogaṃ ca devo makarakuṇḍalemauliṃ padaṃ pārameṣṭhyaṃ sarvalokābhayaṅkaram
translation: The God wears the systems of Sāṅkhya and Yoga as the alligator-shaped ear-rings. He puts on as diadem the highest position of god Brahmā which dispels the fear of all people and affords protection to the world.

devanagari: अव्याकृतमनन्ताख्यमासनं यदधिष्ठितः । धर्मज्ञानादिभिर्युक्तं सत्त्वं पद्ममिहोच्यते ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: avyākṛtamanantākhyamāsanaṃ yadadhiṣṭhitaḥdharmajñānādibhiryuktaṃ sattvaṃ padmamihocyate
translation: The seat called Ananta (serpent Śeṣa) occupied by him is the Unmanifest Prakṛti. His lotus of seat constituted of Sattva guṇa which includes—the six divine excellences, viz. dharma (piety), Jñāna (knowledge), Vairāgya (renunciation, non-attachment) aiśvarya (universal dominion), Śrī (affluence) and yaśas (glory) and of divine powers (known as Vimalā, Utkarṣiṇī, Jñāna, Kriyā, Yoga, Pahvī, Satya, Īśāna and Anugraha).

devanagari: ओजःसहोबलयुतं मुख्यतत्त्वं गदां दधत् । अपां तत्त्वं दरवरं तेजस्तत्त्वं सुदर्शनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ojaḥsahobalayutaṃ mukhyatattvaṃ gadāṃ dadhatapāṃ tattvaṃ daravaraṃ tejastattvaṃ sudarśanam
translation: He wields the principle of mukhya-prāṇa or vital air accompanied by energy, fortitude and strength as his mace; the excellent conch symbolises the principle of water; his discus Sudarśana is the principle of power or fire; His sword clear like the sky is the principle of ether; a shield represents Tamas; His Śārṅga bow stands for the Time-spirit (Kāla) and the quiver (of arrows) consists of the principle of Karma (action).

devanagari: नभोनिभं नभस्तत्त्वमसिं चर्म तमोमयम् । कालरूपं धनुः शार्ङ्गं तथा कर्ममयेषुधिम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: nabhonibhaṃ nabhastattvamasiṃ carma tamomayamkālarūpaṃ dhanuḥ śārṅgaṃ tathā karmamayeṣudhim
translation: He wields the principle of mukhya-prāṇa or vital air accompanied by energy, fortitude and strength as his mace; the excellent conch symbolises the principle of water; his discus Sudarśana is the principle of power or fire; His sword clear like the sky is the principle of ether; a shield represents Tamas; His Śārṅga bow stands for the Time-spirit (Kāla) and the quiver (of arrows) consists of the principle of Karma (action).

devanagari: इन्द्रियाणि शरानाहुराकूतीरस्य स्यन्दनम् । तन्मात्राण्यस्याभिव्यक्तिं मुद्रयार्थक्रियात्मताम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: indriyāṇi śarānāhurākūtīrasya syandanamtanmātrāṇyasyābhivyaktiṃ mudrayārthakriyātmatām
translation: [1] They (i.e. learned experts in traditional lore) state that the (cosmic) sense-organs are his arrows; the (cosmic) mind capable of activation is his Chariot; the subtle elements (tanmātrās) form the exterior of his Chariot; it is by particular positions of his fingers and hands (technically called mudrās) such as varadā, abhayadā) that he confers blessings or gives protection.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī Regards Pṛthvī (the earth) as the chariot of Hari and the five objects of senses are his manifestations. The objects of conative organs are his physical activities.
Anvitārthaprakāśikā is not clear on the function of tanmātrās.

devanagari: मण्डलं देवयजनं दीक्षा संस्कार आत्मनः । परिचर्या भगवत आत्मनो दुरितक्षयः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: maṇḍalaṃ devayajanaṃ dīkṣā saṃskāra ātmanaḥparicaryā bhagavata ātmano duritakṣayaḥ
translation: [2] The particular place where the Lord is worshipped should be conceived as the orb of the sun or the inner Agni’s sphere; initiation by a preceptor by imparting a mantra is the self-consecration rendering one eligible to worship the Lord; and the act of his worship is the means of absolving one’s self of all sins.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī: The spot of Hari’s worship should be Cakrābja and other sacred sport: the real consecration is the purification resulting from God’s presence in the body; the worship of God is the means for absolving one’s self of all sins.

devanagari: भगवान्भगशब्दार्थं लीलाकमलमुद्वहन् । धर्मं यशश्च भगवांश्चामरव्यजनेऽभजत् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavānbhagaśabdārthaṃ līlākamalamudvahandharmaṃ yaśaśca bhagavāṃścāmaravyajane'bhajat
translation: (After explaining the ‘Lotus-seat’, the significance -of the lotus play-fully held on the hand is explained. The Lord (Bhagavān) holds in his hand a lotus sportively; the lotus concretely represents the six characteristic implied in the term bhaga in Bhagavān[3]. The Lord has (by his side attendants holding) a chowrie and a fan representing Dharma and glory respectively.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
The six distinguishing characteristics are enumerated in the following traditional verse:
aiśvaryasya samagrasya dharmasya yaśasaḥ śriyaḥ /
jñāna- vairāgyayoścaiva ṣaṇṇām bhaga itīranā //
They are: Rulership, righteousness or religiosity, glory, affluence, wisdom and non-attachment.

devanagari: आतपत्रं तु वैकुण्ठं द्विजा धामाकुतोभयम् । त्रिवृद्वेदः सुपर्णाख्यो यज्ञं वहति पूरुषम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ātapatraṃ tu vaikuṇṭhaṃ dvijā dhāmākutobhayamtrivṛdvedaḥ suparṇākhyo yajñaṃ vahati pūruṣam
translation: O Brāhmaṇas! The umbrella held over his head stands for his abode Vaikuṇṭha, the absolutely safe place (with no fear from Kāla, Karma, etc.). The Veda with its three divisions (Ṛgveda, Śāman, Yajus) i.e. the sacrifices etc. prescribed in the Veda represents his carrier Garuḍa[4] who bears Yajña, the Supreme Person (Viṣṇu)
purport:
Footnote [4]:
The vedic authority quoted for this by Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, viz. the mantra ‘suparṇo'si garutmān strivṛtte śiraḥ’. is found in Tait. Saṃhitā 4: I: 10: 5 (Vaj. Saṃhitā 12: 4 and a number of Vedic texts.

devanagari: अनपायिनी भगवती श्रीः साक्षादात्मनो हरेः । विष्वक्सेनस्तन्त्रमूर्तिर्विदितः पार्षदाधिपः । नन्दादयोऽष्टौ द्वाःस्थाश्च तेऽणिमाद्या हरेर्ग...
english_devnagari: anapāyinī bhagavatī śrīḥ sākṣādātmano hareḥviṣvaksenastantramūrtirviditaḥ pārṣadādhipaḥnandādayo'ṣṭau dvāḥsthāśca te'ṇimādyā harerguṇāḥ
translation: The inseparable energy or lustre of Lord Hari is the glorious Śrī, the goddess of affluence and beauty. Viśvaksena, the well-known commandant of Viṣṇu’s attendants is the concrete representation of Pañcarātra and other Agamic works, while the (eight) superhuman powers or attributes like aṇimā (capacity to assume atomic size) and others are the attendants at his door like Nanda (Sunanda, etc).

devanagari: वासुदेवः सङ्कर्षणः प्रद्युम्नः पुरुषः स्वयम् । अनिरुद्ध इति ब्रह्मन्मूर्तिव्यूहोऽभिधीयते ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vāsudevaḥ saṅkarṣaṇaḥ pradyumnaḥ puruṣaḥ svayamaniruddha iti brahmanmūrtivyūho'bhidhīyate
translation: [5] The Supreme Person (Nārāyaṇa) himself is manifested as Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna and Aniruddha. He is to be worshipped and meditated in these manifestations[6].
purport:
Footnote [5]:
(The doctrine of Vyūhas, manifestation of the Lord and their worship is now explained—SR.)

Footnote [6]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā explains that the following Vyūhas preside over the following states:
(1) Vāsudeva: Emancipated stage (Mukti-avasṭhā).
(2) Saṅkarṣaṇa: Dreaming state.
(3) Pradyumna: Wakefulness.
(4) Aniruddha: Dreamless deep sleep.
(5) Śrīnārāyaṇa eva svayam mūrtibhedair upāsyaḥ—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: स विश्वस्तैजसः प्राज्ञस्तुरीय इति वृत्तिभिः । अर्थेन्द्रियाशयज्ञानैर्भगवान्परिभाव्यते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa viśvastaijasaḥ prājñasturīya iti vṛttibhiḥarthendriyāśayajñānairbhagavānparibhāvyate
translation: That glorious Lord is to be contemplated (within one’s own self) as Viśva, Taijasa, Prājña and the Fourth (ātman or Vāsudeva) associated with the states of wakefulness, dreaming, dreamless sleep and self-realisation. It is under aforesaid forms that he guides the functions connected with external objects, the mind, the false notions or ignorance and the knowledge that stands as witness to them.[7]
purport:
Footnote [7]:
The Lord, in the wakeful state, presiding over the Viśva stage, cognises external objects such as touch, sound. In this way this verse can be tabulated as follows:—
(1) Wakefulness—Viśva—artha (external objects)
(2) Dream-state—Taijasa—indriya.
(3) Dreamless sleep—Prājña—manas 
(4) Turīya—The self Jñāna.
  (Self-realization)

devanagari: अङ्गोपाङ्गायुधाकल्पैर्भगवांस्तच्चतुष्टयम् । बिभर्ति स्म चतुर्मूर्तिर्भगवान्हरिरीश्वरः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: aṅgopāṅgāyudhākalpairbhagavāṃstaccatuṣṭayambibharti sma caturmūrtirbhagavānharirīśvaraḥ
translation: In this way the glorious Lord Nārāyaṇa with his foresaid limbs of the body, attendants, weapons and ornaments and in his above mentioned four manifestations (Vyūhas), viz. Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna and Aniruddha, assumes also the four aspects such as Viśva Taijasa and others—Yet he is the same glorious Lord Hari, the controller (of the universe).[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā thinks that the above mentioned four forms of Nārāyaṇa are for the creation, protection, dissolution and liberation of the world. Thus Aniruddha creates, Pradyumna protects, Saṅkarṣaṇa destroys and Vāsudeva liberates the world. Bhaktamanorañjanī repeats the same.
Padaratnāvalī: aṅgas—the four forms of the Lord.
Upāṅgas—God Brahmā and other gods in whom the Lord’s presence is prominent.

devanagari: द्विजभ स एष ब्रह्मयोनिः स्वयंदृक् । स्वमहिमपरिपूर्णो मायया च स्वयैतत् । सृजति हरति पातीत्याख्ययानावृताक्षो । विवृत इव निरुक्तस्तत्परैर...
english_devnagari: dvijabha sa eṣa brahmayoniḥ svayaṃdṛksvamahimaparipūrṇo māyayā ca svayaitatsṛjati harati pātītyākhyayānāvṛtākṣovivṛta iva niruktastatparairātmalabhyaḥ
translation: O prominent Brāhmaṇa! The above-described Lord is the source of the Vedas, the self-illuminating, omniscient, perfect in his own glory and essential blissfulness. It is (out of sportiveness) that under different designations (such, as Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra) that he creates, protects and dissolves the universe by his own Māyā. But his consciousness remains unobstructed and unclouded. Though he is described (in Śāstras) as differentiated, he is really one and is realized as such by his votaries as being their own self.

devanagari: श्रीकृष्ण कृष्णसख वृष्ण्यृषभावनिध्रुग् । राजन्यवंशदहनानपवर्गवीर्य । गोविन्द गोपवनिताव्रजभृत्यगीत । तीर्थश्रवः श्रवणमङ्गल पाहि भृत्यान्...
english_devnagari: śrīkṛṣṇa kṛṣṇasakha vṛṣṇyṛṣabhāvanidhrugrājanyavaṃśadahanānapavargavīryagovinda gopavanitāvrajabhṛtyagītatīrthaśravaḥ śravaṇamaṅgala pāhi bhṛtyān
translation: O Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the friend of Arjuna, the prominent leader of Vṛṣṇis, the fire unto the families of Kṣattriyas who were inimical to (and a burden) to the earth; O Lord of undiminishing energy and prowess; O Govinda (Protector of cows), whose hallowing glory is sung by the cowherd women of Vraja and servants i.e. devotees like Nārada and whose name and deeds are auspicious to hear! O Lord, be pleased to protect us, your servants (Votaries).

devanagari: य इदं कल्य उत्थाय महापुरुषलक्षणम् । तच्चित्तः प्रयतो जप्त्वा ब्रह्म वेद गुहाशयम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ya idaṃ kalya utthāya mahāpuruṣalakṣaṇamtaccittaḥ prayato japtvā brahma veda guhāśayam
translation: He who, rising early at dawn, purifies himself by taking a bath and with concentrated mind repeats the above- mentioned characteristics (of aṅga, upāṅga, etc.) of the Supreme Person, realizes Brahman as abiding in his own heart.

devanagari: श्रीशौनक उवाच । शुको यदाह भगवान्विष्णुराताय शृण्वते । सौरो गणो मासि मासि नाना वसति सप्तकः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśaunaka uvācaśuko yadāha bhagavānviṣṇurātāya śṛṇvatesauro gaṇo māsi māsi nānā vasati saptakaḥ
translation:
Śaunaka said:
As the glorious Śuka told the listening Parīkṣit (one protected by Viṣṇu) that a group of seven (pairs) consisting of sages, Gandharvas, Apsaras, Nāgas, Yakṣas and Rākṣasas and presided over by the sun-god, carry out their duties each, month (and are substituted by a new batch next month.)[9]

devanagari: तेषां नामानि कर्माणि नियुक्तानामधीश्वरैः । ब्रूहि नः श्रद्दधानानां व्यूहं सूर्यात्मनो हरेः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ nāmāni karmāṇi niyuktānāmadhīśvaraiḥbrūhi naḥ śraddadhānānāṃ vyūhaṃ sūryātmano hareḥ
translation: Be pleased to recount to us who are devout and faithful the names, functions of these along with their presiding deities and of Hari’s manifestation as the indwellers of the sun-god.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । अनाद्यविद्यया विष्णोरात्मनः सर्वदेहिनाम् । निर्मितो लोकतन्त्रोऽयं लोकेषु परिवर्तते ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācaanādyavidyayā viṣṇorātmanaḥ sarvadehināmnirmito lokatantro'yaṃ lokeṣu parivartate
translation:
Sūṭa said:
This (visible) sun-god who controls the world (by maintaining the course of life iṅ it) and revolves in his orbit, is created by the beginningless Māyā of Lord Viṣṇu who abides as an indwelling soul of all embodied beings.

devanagari: एक एव हि लोकानां सूर्य आत्मादिकृद्धरिः । सर्ववेदक्रियामूलमृषिभिर्बहुधोदितः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: eka eva hi lokānāṃ sūrya ātmādikṛddhariḥsarvavedakriyāmūlamṛṣibhirbahudhoditaḥ
translation: Though one without a second, it is Lord Hari, the creator of the world, the one soul of all people who is the sungod. He is spoken under diverse names by the sages as he is the source of different Vedic rites.

devanagari: कालो देशः क्रिया कर्ता करणं कार्यमागमः । द्रव्यं फलमिति ब्रह्मन्नवधोक्तोऽजया हरिः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kālo deśaḥ kriyā kartā karaṇaṃ kāryamāgamaḥdravyaṃ phalamiti brahmannavadhokto'jayā hariḥ
translation: O Brāhmaṇa Śaunaka! It is due to Hari’s beginningless Māyā that Hari is spoken of in nine forms, viz. time, place activity, the doer, the instrument, duty or performance, sacred book, material substance (rice, etc.) and the fruit (viz. celestial world, etc.)

devanagari: मध्वादिषु द्वादशसु भगवान्कालरूपधृक् । लोकतन्त्राय चरति पृथग्द्वादशभिर्गणैः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: madhvādiṣu dvādaśasu bhagavānkālarūpadhṛklokatantrāya carati pṛthagdvādaśabhirgaṇaiḥ
translation: In order to help the conduct and functions of the world, he assumes the form of Time (out of the nine mentioned above) and goes through his course in twelve months beginning with Caitra along with his twelve groups of attendants.

devanagari: धाता१ कृतस्थली हेतिर्वासुकी रथकृन्मुने । पुलस्त्यस्तुम्बुरुरिति मधुमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: dhātā1 kṛtasthalī hetirvāsukī rathakṛnmunepulastyastumbururiti madhumāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation:
The sun-god, under the designation, Dhātā, Kṛtasthalī (the heavenly damsel), Heti, (the demon), Vāsuki (the serpent), Rathakṛt (the Yakṣa), Pulastya (the Sage) and Tumbaru (the Gandharva)—all these run the world in the first month (Caitra) or Madhu.
(In the following verses the names of the sun and his attendants are given in the same order as in verse 33 above).

devanagari: अर्यमा पुलहोऽथौजाः प्रहेतिः पुञ्जिकस्थली । नारदः कच्छनीरश्च नयन्त्येते स्म माधवम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: aryamā pulaho'thaujāḥ prahetiḥ puñjikasthalīnāradaḥ kacchanīraśca nayantyete sma mādhavam
translation: Aryamā, Puñjikasthalī, Praheti, Kachhanīra, Athaujā, Pulaha and Nārada—these run the month of Vaiśākha or (Mādhava).

devanagari: मित्रोऽत्रिः पौरुषेयोऽथ तक्षको मेनका हहाः । रथस्वन इति ह्येते शुक्रमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: mitro'triḥ pauruṣeyo'tha takṣako menakā hahāḥrathasvana iti hyete śukramāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Puṣā, Ghṛtācī, Vāta, Dhanañjaya, Suruci, Gautama and Suṣeṇa—these run the month of Māgha. (or Tapomāsa).

devanagari: वसिष्ठो वरुणो रम्भा सहजन्यस्तथा हुहूः । शुक्रश्चित्रस्वनश्चैव शुचिमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vasiṣṭho varuṇo rambhā sahajanyastathā huhūḥśukraścitrasvanaścaiva śucimāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Varuṇa, Rambhā, Chitrasvana [Citrasvana?], Śukra, Sahajanya, Vasiṣṭha and Huhū [Hūhū?]—these run the month of Āṣāḍha (or Śucimāsa).

devanagari: इन्द्रो विश्वावसुः श्रोता एलापत्रस्तथाङ्गिराः । प्रम्लोचा राक्षसो वर्यो नभोमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: indro viśvāvasuḥ śrotā elāpatrastathāṅgirāḥpramlocā rākṣaso varyo nabhomāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Indra, Pramlocā, Varya, Elāpatra, Śrotā, Aṅgirā and Viśvāvasu—these run the month of Srāvaṇa [=Śrāvaṇa?] (or Nabhomāsa),

devanagari: विवस्वानुग्रसेनश्च व्याघ्र आसारणो भृगुः । अनुम्लोचा शङ्खपालो नभस्याख्यं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: vivasvānugrasenaśca vyāghra āsāraṇo bhṛguḥanumlocā śaṅkhapālo nabhasyākhyaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Vivasvān, Anumlocā, Vyāghra, Śaṅkhapāla, Āsāraṇa Bhṛgu and Ugrasena—these run the month called Bhādrapada or (Nabhasya).

devanagari: पूषा धनञ्जयो वातः सुषेणः सुरुचिस्तथा । घृताची गौतमश्चेति तपोमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: pūṣā dhanañjayo vātaḥ suṣeṇaḥ surucistathāghṛtācī gautamaśceti tapomāsaṃ nayantyamī

devanagari: ऋतुर्वर्चा भरद्वाजः पर्जन्यः सेनजित्तथा । विश्व ऐरावतश्चैव तपस्याख्यं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ṛturvarcā bharadvājaḥ parjanyaḥ senajittathāviśva airāvataścaiva tapasyākhyaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Parjanya, Senajit, Varcā, Airāvata, Kratu, Bharadvāja and Viśva—these run the month named Phālguna or (Tapasya).

devanagari: अथांशुः कश्यपस्तार्क्ष्य ऋतसेनस्तथोर्वशी । विद्युच्छत्रुर्महाशङ्खः सहोमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: athāṃśuḥ kaśyapastārkṣya ṛtasenastathorvaśīvidyucchatrurmahāśaṅkhaḥ sahomāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: [10] Again, Aṃśu, Urvaśī, Vidyucchatru, Mahāśaṅkha Tārkṣya, Kaśyapa and Ṛtasena—these run the month of Mārgaśīrṣa (or Sahomāsa).
purport:
Footnote [10]:
There is a change in the usual order of the names of these months. Bhāvāratha Dīpikā attributes it to the change of Kalpa though the explanation is not convincing, so do Anvitārthaprakāśikā and Bālaprabodhini follow SR. Bhāgavata Candrikā and Bhaktamanorañjanī suggest us to adjust the order of ślokas.

devanagari: भगः स्फूर्जोऽरिष्टनेमिरूर्ण आयुश्च पञ्चमः । कर्कोटकः पूर्वचित्तिः पुष्यमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagaḥ sphūrjo'riṣṭanemirūrṇa āyuśca pañcamaḥkarkoṭakaḥ pūrvacittiḥ puṣyamāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Bhaga, Pūrvacitti, Sphūrja, Karkoṭaka, Ūrṇa, Āyu and Ariṣṭanemi—these run the month of Pauṣa (or Puṣyamāsa).

devanagari: त्वष्टा ऋचीकतनयः कम्बलश्च तिलोत्तमा । ब्रह्मापेतोऽथ शतजिद्धृतराष्ट्र इषम्भराः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṣṭā ṛcīkatanayaḥ kambalaśca tilottamābrahmāpeto'tha śatajiddhṛtarāṣṭra iṣambharāḥ
translation: Again, Tvaṣṭā, Tilottamā, Brahmāpeta, Kambala, Śatajit, Jamadagni and Dhṛtarāṣṭra are the presiding set in the month of Āśvina. (Iṣambhara]).

devanagari: विष्णुरश्वतरो रम्भा सूर्यवर्चाश्च सत्यजित् । विश्वामित्रो मखापेत ऊर्जमासं नयन्त्यमी ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇuraśvataro rambhā sūryavarcāśca satyajitviśvāmitro makhāpeta ūrjamāsaṃ nayantyamī
translation: Viṣṇu, Rambhā, Makhāpeta, Aśvatara, Satyajit, Visvāmitra [Viśvāmitra?] and Sūryavarca—these run the month of Kārttika (or Ūrjamāsa).

devanagari: एता भगवतो विष्णोरादित्यस्य विभूतयः । स्मरतां सन्ध्ययोर्नॄणां हरन्त्यंहो दिने दिने ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: etā bhagavato viṣṇorādityasya vibhūtayaḥsmaratāṃ sandhyayornṝṇāṃ harantyaṃho dine dine
translation: These constitute the glory of the sun-god, who is no other than Lord Viṣṇu himself. Those who remember them both in the morning and in the evening, day after day are rid of their sins by them.

devanagari: द्वादशस्वपि मासेषु देवोऽसौ षड्भिरस्य वै । चरन्समन्तात्तनुते परत्रेह च सन्मतिम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: dvādaśasvapi māseṣu devo'sau ṣaḍbhirasya vaicaransamantāttanute paratreha ca sanmatim
translation: This celebrated sun-god courses round this world in each of the twelve months (of the year) along with his attendants (mentioned above), aṇd promotes and expands goodness of the mind (or intellect helping people to perform religious rites), securing their happiness here and bliss hereafter (This reminds one of the Gāyatrī mantra).

devanagari: सामर्ग्यजुर्भिस्तल्लिङ्गैरृषयः संस्तुवन्त्यमुम् । गन्धर्वास्तं प्रगायन्ति नृत्यन्त्यप्सरसोऽग्रतः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: sāmargyajurbhistalliṅgairṛṣayaḥ saṃstuvantyamumgandharvāstaṃ pragāyanti nṛtyantyapsaraso'grataḥ
translation: Sages who attend the sun-god (as members of his entourage) praise him with the hymns from the Sāmaveda, Ṛg- Veda and Yajur-veda pertaining to his glory. Gandharvas sing songs (in praise of him). The celestial damsels dance before him.

devanagari: उन्नह्यन्ति रथं नागा ग्रामण्यो रथयोजकाः । चोदयन्ति रथं पृष्ठे नैरृता बलशालिनः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: unnahyanti rathaṃ nāgā grāmaṇyo rathayojakāḥcodayanti rathaṃ pṛṣṭhe nairṛtā balaśālinaḥ
translation: The Nāgas (Serpents) bind the chariot fast (like cords), Yakṣas make the chariot ready; the mighty Rākṣasas push the chariot from behind.

devanagari: वालखिल्याः सहस्राणि षष्टिर्ब्रह्मर्षयोऽमलाः । पुरतोऽभिमुखं यान्ति स्तुवन्ति स्तुतिभिर्विभुम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: vālakhilyāḥ sahasrāṇi ṣaṣṭirbrahmarṣayo'malāḥpurato'bhimukhaṃ yānti stuvanti stutibhirvibhum
translation: (The group of) sixty thousand pure-minded (tiny) Vālakhilya sages (who are always with the sun-god all the year round) proceed ahead of him and extol the Almighty- God with panegyrical hymns.

devanagari: एवं ह्यनादिनिधनो भगवान्हरिरीश्वरः । कल्पे कल्पे स्वमात्मानं व्यूह्य लोकानवत्यजः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ hyanādinidhano bhagavānharirīśvaraḥkalpe kalpe svamātmānaṃ vyūhya lokānavatyajaḥ
translation:
In this way, the eternal (beginningless and endless), birthless, glorious Lord Hari divides himself (into twelve forms, each presiding over the sun (for the period of one month) and protects the world in every Kalpa.
[back to top]
